content:
If you find this story paywalled, please consider subscribing to Medium to support both the platform and the creator. Otherwise, you can find this same piece on my newsletter: Citizens are more ready for government AI than governments realize (offsite link)
Every two years, the Boston Consulting Group does a massive survey around the world about the digital services governments provide to people. The 2026 edition of this survey just came out, and it covers 44 countries, representing 70% of the world’s population. The results are interesting, and if you’re a government official, you probably won’t like them.
People are actually more comfortable with AI being used in government than governments think they are. In many countries, people want AI to make decisions instead of government officials because.. you know how government offices work.
And people are not happy with government digital services. As technology accelerates, governments are not keeping up with it. This has resulted in a drop of 13 percentage points in the overall satisfaction rate. The number of…
